title: Bridge Essay: Global neural self-disturbance in schizophrenia: A systematic fMRI review.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Nondual/Unity** (Phase 16) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Nondual/Unity to mechanistic neuroscience through Global, neural, self-disturbance, in, schizophrenia:.

**Global neural self-disturbance in schizophrenia: Asystematic fMRI review.** — Schizophr Res. PMID: 38820980. DOI: 10.1016/j.schres.2024.05.015. There is ageneral consensus that schizophrenia (SZ) is characterized by major changes in the sense of self. Phenomenological studies suggest that these changes in the sense of self stem from abasic disturbance, hence the term 'basic self-disturbance'. While imaging studies demonstrate changes in various regions during self-focused tasks, the exact neural correlates of such basic self-disturbances remain unclear. If the self-disturbance is indeed basic and thereby underlies all other symptoms,
